findings.title: No baseline (SeqLoRA, O-LoRA, PerTaskFT, EWC, LwF, …) code in repo
findings.quote:
## Models and Usage

### T5 Model

Training and evaluation scripts are in `scripts/`, outputs and logs are in `logs_and_outputs/`.
findings.claim: The repo ships only the DEAL training/eval pipeline (scripts/, scripts_llama/, src/*_run_wavelet.py). There is no script, config, or module that produces the SeqLoRA, O-LoRA, PerTaskFT (Table 1) or EWC/LwF/L2P/Replay/ProgPrompt/LFPT5/LB-CL (Table 10) numbers.
findings.concern: Every comparison in Table 1 and Table 10 — the basis for the 'consistently outperforms baselines' headline claim — relies on baseline numbers that no code in the repo computes, so the central comparison is not reproducible from this artefact.
findings.resolution: Authors: please add the baseline training/eval scripts and configs (or point to the exact upstream commit/command used), so the reported baseline AA/R-1 can be reproduced under the identical split/metric/budget.
findings.reason: README quote confirmed at line 66-70; grepping the entire repo for SeqLoRA/O-LoRA/EWC/LwF/PerTaskFT/ProgPrompt returns no implementation — only a stray path string mentioning 'O-LoRA' in a comment.

findings.title: Claimed two-sided t-test (p<0.05) significance has no code and no per-run logs
findings.quote:
Note. Bold indicates the statistically significant improvements
(i.e., two-sided t-test with p < 0.05) over the best baseline.
findings.claim: Table 1 and Table 4 mark results as significant via a two-sided t-test; the repo contains no statistical-test code (no scipy.stats/ttest anywhere) and ships no per-seed/per-run scores. The run scripts never pass --seed, so a single default-seed run is produced per task, providing no sample for a t-test.
findings.concern: The significance annotation (which underwrites the 'statistically significant improvement over the best baseline' claim) cannot be reproduced or verified from the artefact, and a single deterministic run cannot support a t-test.
findings.resolution: Authors: provide the per-run scores, the number of runs/seeds, and the t-test script (sample definition, paired vs unpaired, one/two-sided).
findings.reason: Paper text confirms t-test claim at lines 545-546 and 686; no scipy/ttest/t_test anywhere in the codebase; no --seed argument in any of the three scripts (TC.sh, standard.sh, long.sh).

findings.title: Adapter-update (Fig 2a), kernel (Table 3) and efficiency (Tab 8/9) ablations absent
findings.claim: The code unconditionally trains lora_A and freezes lora_B; there is no flag to select 'Only B' or 'Both A&B' (Fig 2a), no alternative-kernel implementation (Table 3 only ships wave='haar'), and no timing/memory profiling (Tables 8/9).
findings.concern: Fig 2a's 'Both A&B = 75.6' (the chosen/best configuration), Table 3's kernel comparison, and Tables 8/9 efficiency numbers cannot be reproduced because the code path they describe is not present.
findings.resolution: Authors: add the configuration switches and kernel variants used for Fig 2a / Table 3, and the profiling script for Tables 8/9.
findings.reason: Quote confirmed at T5_run_wavelet.py lines 328-333 (lora_B frozen unconditionally); wavelet is always 'haar' (line 351); no profiling scripts found in repo.

findings.title: Eq.12 regularization silently never applied (isinstance against wrong class)
findings.claim: The trainer guards the regularization loop with `isinstance(module, LinearWaveletFilter)` where `LinearWaveletFilter` is imported from `waveletLoRAAdapter` (line 12). But the modules actually inserted into the model are instances of the *separate* `LinearWaveletFilter` class defined inside `T5_run_wavelet.py` (line 137) / `Llama3_run_wavelet.py` (line 139); those run scripts never import the adapter-module class. The two classes are distinct objects, so `isinstance` is always False, the loop body never runs, and `reg_loss` stays 0.
findings.concern: The paper's 'Controlled Knowledge Updating' contribution — the λ1‖θ‖^a + λ2‖MLP‖^b term of Eq.12 and the asymmetric a≥b regularization that Table 4 grid-searches — is never present in the loss, so a core advertised component is inert and Table 4 (AA varying 74.8→85.5 with (a,b)) cannot be produced by this code.
findings.resolution: Authors: confirm whether reg was active in the reported runs; if so, import the same LinearWaveletFilter class in both trainer and run script (or unify on one definition) and re-verify that reg_loss is non-zero during training.
findings.reason: Check script run and saved to _verifier_code/out/reg_isinstance.txt; output confirms VERDICT = True: trainer imports LinearWaveletFilter from waveletLoRAAdapter (line 12), both T5 and Llama3 run-scripts define and instantiate their own separate LinearWaveletFilter class without importing from waveletLoRAAdapter, so isinstance() is always False and reg_loss stays 0.

findings.title: scripts/long.sh (T5 15-task) crashes: FLAN-T5 path matches no branch in Llama script
findings.quote:
model_path="google/flan-t5-large"

for i in "${!datasets[@]}"; do
  dataset="${datasets[$i]}"
  round=$((i + 1))
  output_dir="logs_and_outputs/long/outputs/${round}-${dataset}"

  python src/Llama3_run_wavelet.py \
findings.claim: scripts/long.sh runs the T5 15-task benchmark but invokes src/Llama3_run_wavelet.py with model_name_or_path=google/flan-t5-large. In that script the model/tokenizer are only assigned under `if 'adapter' in path` or `elif 'llama' in path` (lines 283-341); 'google/flan-t5-large' matches neither on the first task, so config/tokenizer/model are never bound and execution raises UnboundLocalError/NameError before training.
findings.concern: The T5 15-task headline result (Table 1: T5+DEAL AA 73.9 / R-1 79.1) cannot be reproduced with the provided script, since it fails on the very first task.
findings.resolution: Authors: provide the script actually used for the T5 15-task run (presumably T5_run_wavelet.py with the long_configs), or add a base-model branch in Llama3_run_wavelet.py.
findings.reason: long.sh confirmed at lines 10-17: model_path='google/flan-t5-large' passed to Llama3_run_wavelet.py; Llama3 script (lines 283-341) only assigns config/tokenizer/model under 'adapter' or 'llama' branches — 'flan-t5-large' matches neither, so variables are unbound and execution crashes before training.

findings.title: Code uses a single-level Haar DWT gate, not the SVD-truncation + heat-kernel network of §3.2
findings.quote:
self.dwt = DWTForward(J=1, wave=self.wavelet, mode='zero').to(device)
self.idwt = DWTInverse(wave=self.wavelet, mode='zero').to(device)
weight_4d = self.original_weight.unsqueeze(0).unsqueeze(0).to(device)
cA, _ = self.dwt(weight_4d)
cA_shape = cA.shape
self.theta = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(cA_shape, device=device))
findings.claim: The 'Wavelet Kernel-based Knowledge Retention' module is implemented as a single-level (J=1) Haar discrete wavelet transform of the lora_A weight matrix with an element-wise learnable gate `theta` on the approximation coefficients cA, followed (in forward, lines 184-196) by an inverse DWT and an MLP. No singular value decomposition, no truncated-SVD core-feature estimate (Eq.6-7), and no multi-scale heat-kernel wavelet network (Eq.8-10) appear anywhere in the repo (grep for svd/singular/heat/eckart returns nothing in src).
findings.concern: The paper's central retention mechanism, derived in §3.2 as SVD-truncation denoising filtered by a series of learnable heat kernels at multiple scales, is a different operation from the implemented single-level Haar DWT gate, so the method evaluated is not the method described.
findings.resolution: Authors: clarify whether the reported results use the Haar-DWT implementation or the SVD/heat-kernel derivation in §3.2, and reconcile the paper's Eq.7-10 with the released code.
findings.reason: Quote confirmed at T5_run_wavelet.py lines 159-164 (DWTForward J=1, wave=haar); grep for 'svd', 'singular', 'heat' across all src/ files returns zero hits; Llama3_run_wavelet.py has an identical DWT-based class at line 139; waveletLoRAAdapter.py also has no SVD/heat logic.

findings.title: Code always trains A and freezes B, but paper's default/best is 'Both A & B'
findings.claim: On every continual-learning step the code unfreezes lora_A and freezes lora_B (the wavelet/MLP module replaces lora_A only; lora_B is passed through unchanged in replace_lora_params). This is the 'Only Adapter A' configuration.
findings.concern: Figure 2a reports 'Both A & B' (75.6 AA) as the highest and presents it as the adopted strategy, while the released code implements 'Only A' (reported as 72.8); the main-table runs therefore use a strategy the ablation says is sub-optimal, or the released code differs from what produced Table 1.
findings.resolution: Authors: confirm which adapter-update strategy produced the Table 1 numbers and, if 'Both A&B', release that code path.
findings.reason: Quote confirmed at T5_run_wavelet.py lines 328-333; lora_B is unconditionally frozen and lora_A unconditionally unfrozen; no flag or branch exists for 'Both A&B'; the same pattern is present in Llama3_run_wavelet.py.

findings.title: README says 1,000 train / 500 val per task; shipped data + scripts use far more
findings.quote: *All datasets are reformulated into an instruction-following format, with each task limited to 1,000 training and 500 validation examples. For full details on dataset preprocessing, task ordering, and prompt construction, see the Appendix in our paper.*
findings.claim: The README claims 1,000 train / 500 val per task, but the shipped CL_Benchmark/*/train.json hold 250-14,000 examples (e.g. dbpedia 14,000, yahoo 10,000, amazon 5,000) and the scripts set neither --max_train_samples nor --max_num_instances_per_task below its 10,000 default, so up to 10,000 train examples per task are actually used.
findings.concern: The 'data-efficient / small-scale dataset' framing central to the paper's motivation is not enforced by the released configuration, so the data budget actually used is much larger than stated.
findings.resolution: Authors: clarify the train/val sizes used for the reported numbers and set the corresponding caps in the scripts (or trim the data files).
findings.reason: README quote confirmed at line 62; CL_Benchmark/TC/dbpedia/train.json confirmed to contain 14,000 records; T5_run_wavelet.py line 90 sets max_num_instances_per_task default to 10,000; no script passes --max_num_instances_per_task or --max_train_samples to cap it at 1,000.
